title: FileType enum uses static-str payload to carry mime type — primitive obsession
description:
## Severity
Trivial

## Dimension
SOLID-O (OCP) / Decoupling

## Location
src/tools/file/read.rs:10-37 (FileType enum and detect_file_type)

## Violation
```rust
pub enum FileType {
    Text,
    Image(&'static str),  // mime type
    Pdf,
    Notebook,
}
```

The Image variant carries its mime type as a raw `&'static str`. The dispatcher in mod.rs (line 206) then passes that opaque string down to read_image_file. Adding a new image format (e.g. AVIF) requires editing detect_file_type, the read_image_file signature, the dispatcher, and probably a hidden assumption in the Anthropic adapter that expects "image/png" or "image/jpeg". OCP violation: the enum is not closed under extension.

The trailing comment "// mime type" carries the type information the type system should be carrying. Use a typed Mime instead.

## Mandated refactor
```rust
pub enum ImageKind { Png, Jpeg, Gif, Webp }
impl ImageKind {
    pub const fn mime(self) -> &'static str { ... }
    pub fn from_extension(ext: &str) -> Option<Self> { ... }
}
pub enum FileType { Text, Image(ImageKind), Pdf, Notebook }
```

This forces every consumer to handle the enum exhaustively and makes adding AVIF a single edit-and-recompile.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
